_comment: Reference schema for project-context/context.json. This is the single source of truth for the business-context contract every consumer agent reads. Consumers MUST read defensively: a missing field means ask the user or omit, never hard-error. Do not add consumer-specific fields here; per-run parameters belong in each agent's own config.json.

one_liner.description: One sentence: what it is and who it's for.

category.description: Market category in the words a buyer would search, e.g. 'freelance invoicing tools'.

pains.description: Real frustrations, ideally in the buyer's words.

jobs.description: Jobs-to-be-done they are hiring the product for.

proof.description: Evidence for the promise. Each item should trace to something real (a number with a source, a named feature, a press mention). See voice.claims_policy.

banned_words.description: Words/phrases to never use in copy.

claims_policy.description: Rule consumers enforce when writing copy. Default: 'no invented stats, every metric must trace to a URL or be omitted'.
